title: lilium albanicum var. jankae
intro: Lilium albanicum var. jankae, also known as Janka's Lily, is a rare and endangered species of the lily family. It is a flowering bulbous perennial plant native to Albania and Greece, known for its white or pale yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the bulbs of Janka's Lily are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures that are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and promoting relaxation.
